All right, welcome back to this lesson. So this is all about how to use ethical urgency in the spirit of what we've been referring to as the 100% close. So to revisit that concept, we aim, we strive to have a 100% decision rate. So a very clear yes or a very clear no from anyone who's been extended an invitation to our program. Right? So this is a standard that we choose to hold both ourselves and our clients to. However, here's the kicker. We invite and allow for whatever spaciousness and time is needed. And paradoxically, it's by depressurizing the situation that our pre-clients can connect back to their sense of expanded vision, possibility, power, and safety to actually achieve and actualize that result that they are desiring. So typically, there could be a lot of pressure in the second part of the sales call when they are faced with the investment options and they're really wrestling with this decision. Right? Even if you are doing everything right, they can be creating their own form of this decision anxiety. Right? And when someone is in anxiety, when someone is an overwhelm, when someone is feeling pressure, this creates a state of incoherence. Right? You literally can't connect back to possibility if you are in overwhelm. You can't connect to a sense of safety if you're in this spinning spiral and afraid of making this investment that rationally speaking actually is quite plausible, quite easy for you to make, very clear in the ROI that you're able to have. Right? So you don't want to prolong the amount of time someone spends in a state of pressure, overwhelm, or anxiety, regardless of whether or not it's coming from you. And it won't be coming from you if you've done everything else we're talking about in this program. But remember, it can be self-created and when it does appear beyond anything else, you want to facilitate their reentry and reestablishing of those three meta-states, even if it means taking a breather from the call. So here's the rule of thumb. If by the 55-minimark, you don't feel like there's clear momentum towards a decision or at any point you feel like they are spinning to a place of overwhelm, anxiety, and self-created pressure, don't be afraid to propose ending the call and creating a follow-up opportunity. This isn't weak. This isn't passive. This is actually leadership in service of creating a context where they will actually be empowered to give a decision, even if it is not on this call itself. So the only reason one would ever be afraid to let a prospect off the call without a decision is if A, they know that the only reason one would say yes is due to pressure and manipulation and essentially creating that disempowered state where forced compliance becomes easy. If that is off the table, then there's no reason to fear letting someone off the call. Or B, if they don't have the adequate follow-up systems in place to coach that decision in the coming days. And this is solvable as well. So as a result, many, not you, will end up going into those hardcore urgency tactics, those expiring discounts, those way too farly exaggerated consequences of what will happen. This false destiny of what will happen if they don't join your program right now, how their situation will deteriorate at this rapid pace and everyone will hate them and life will suck and all this other fear mongering bullshit, which is essentially what you hear on a lot of these sales calls and what we are obviously striving to go into complete opposite direction from. Legitimate urgency should only come from legitimate places such as the number of spots in your program before it reaches capacity. Don't make this up, right? If there are legitimately only three remaining places and there are legitimately only three remaining places and when you state that, you could say it from the place like I just have to be honest and clear. We have three remaining places. I have a few more calls that said I don't want this decision to come from a place of pressure or urgency. I just wanted you to have that information. That's all right. So you're not using it. You're not weaponizing it. You're simply stating the facts. This will also come in the case of live cohorts. If your program has a very clear start date and live coaching sessions, then that is a natural and built urgency. So it's like, I really want to respect your time in making this decision when it feels right to you, but I just want to let you know that this program is starting Monday. We have our first coaching session booked for Wednesday and I really want you to be able to enjoy every component of this program and feel like you're hitting the ground running with everybody else if you decide to move on. So those are two very legitimate, easeful ways for including legitimate urgency and finally it's the actual stakes in their life. Not the hyper exaggerated ones, not the false destiny, but the plausibilities based on what you've heard them share. This is where you would want to know. If you're not going to be working with me, if this isn't the right time for you, what is your alternative path? You have an alternative path towards fixing this problem or pursuing this outcome. You could get clear what is going to continue to happen if this problem persists. What will show up in your life? Have them give that answer. It's not up to you to give a false destiny, but simply ask them. If we didn't move on and you don't really have any other ways of going about this right now, just for your own knowledge and your own self-awareness, what do you feel is likely to happen over the next few days with regards to X problem and have them describe it. Once again, anything they offer to the conversation is going to carry that much more weight than something you propose towards them. Cool. In a case where they are not sure or it is a bit of a maybe later answer, anything that is not an explicit no, it is important that you invest the final stages of your call and helping them become aware of their self-created path and getting permission to follow up. If you are a fitness coach and they say, you know, I'm just not feeling like I'm going to have the time right now. There's a lot going on at work. I'm going to be traveling. I don't think I could stay consistent. That's fine. You're not going to pressure. You're not going to create false urgency, but you will be of service if you say, you know, that's cool. But just so that I make sure you make good on these goals that you've so generously and openly shared with me today. I just want to know what your path is going to be. Right? How are you going to go about this on your own in order to get these results? And maybe they have a self-created path. Maybe for this guy, it would be I'm going to watch some kettlebell videos on YouTube and I'm going to buy a kettlebell and I'm going to do 20 pushups every morning and 20 jump squats every evening. And you could say, well, do you feel good staying consistent with that? Like, is this something you see yourself doing? And they might say, yeah, sure. Like, I really do feel like I could do this and get that result. And then before you get off the call, get permission to follow up. You could say something along the lines of, you know, that's cool. I really do want to honor that. And I really respect and admire your courage to really embark on this solo. A lot of people, obviously, without that accountability, without the confidence in knowing that what they're doing is yielding a result, fall off that path. Right? They're not able to stay consistent. But, you know, I appreciate everything I've heard about you, everything you've shared. And I certainly want to see you succeed with that. Is it cool if we follow up or if I follow up in about two to three weeks from now, just to make sure that things are on track, that things haven't slipped up because I'd really hate to see things backtrack, you know. And in most cases, they're going to give you a clear yes. And at that point, when you follow up in two to three weeks, you're not following up with any kind of pressure, right? You're simply asking them if their self-created path has been effective. So at that point, they will know from a place of actual real-life evidence, not fear mongering on the call whether or not their decision at that point to work with you or not work with you was effective or not. And if it wasn't effective, that is the best opportunity for them to say, you know what, I think now is the right time to jump on board. So all of this, this is the core of what we call the Tyler Durden follow-up strategy that is part of our automated intimacy program. However, below this video somewhere, you should see two lessons we pulled straight from there. It's the Tyler Durden call audit, which I suggest you perform after every sales call, which will help you write down what that self-created path is and when you're going to follow up with them. It's also going to give you extremely valuable guidelines of what you can extract from the sales call itself and perhaps use in your marketing process. And then of course, what that follow-up process is. So we have some bonus lessons on that. If you want the full version of the Tyler Durden follow-up, including all the advanced automations you can use in your active campaign to set reminders, to set, to create notes on someone's contact card to make sure that never gets lost in the shuffle, as well as a host of other incredible playbooks for scaling your coaching and consulting business. There should be a link somewhere around here for you to check that out or you can always follow up with me personally, wherever you prefer to follow up with me, usually on Facebook or by email. So that is it. I super appreciate you going through this course, taking the stand to run your sales calls with a degree of integrity and ethics that just unfortunately hasn't really been experienced in this industry up to now. I encourage you to reach out in the Facebook group as you put this into practice. Reach out to us directly to let us know how this is working for you. If you have any questions that come up along the way, we're certainly here to help you integrate these somewhat advanced concepts into your business, but that once implemented, once integrated, actually start becoming more natural than these rigid scripts that you may have felt like you've had to adhere to in the past. So we're here for you. I'm grateful to have you in this program. I'm grateful to have you putting this into practice. And I look forward to catching you in another program. Much love.
